• Show All Code
  • Hide All Code

Restaurant Complaints vs Inspection Risk in NYC

STA 9750 — Final Individual Report

Mary Ononye

2026-05-06

Setup — Packages

pkgs <- c(
  "tidyverse", "lubridate", "scales",
  "knitr", "kableExtra", "ggcorrplot",
  "broom", "glue"
)
installed  <- rownames(installed.packages())
to_install <- pkgs[!pkgs %in% installed]
if (length(to_install) > 0) install.packages(to_install)

library(tidyverse)
library(lubridate)
library(scales)
library(knitr)
library(kableExtra)
library(ggcorrplot)
library(broom)
library(glue)
theme_nyc <- function() {
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
    theme(
      plot.title       = element_text(face = "bold", size = 15),
      plot.subtitle    = element_text(color = "grey40", size = 12),
      plot.caption     = element_text(color = "grey55", size = 9),
      axis.title       = element_text(size = 11),
      panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
      strip.text       = element_text(face = "bold")
    )
}

borough_colors <- c(
  "BRONX"         = "#E76F51",
  "BROOKLYN"      = "#2A9D8F",
  "MANHATTAN"     = "#264653",
  "QUEENS"        = "#E9C46A",
  "STATEN ISLAND" = "#A8DADC"
)

Data Loading

# Load from locally saved RDS files — no API calls during knit
raw_inspections <- readRDS("data/raw_inspections.rds")
raw_311         <- readRDS("data/raw_311.rds")

cat("Inspections rows:", nrow(raw_inspections), "\n")
## Inspections rows: 288676
cat("311 complaint rows:", nrow(raw_311), "\n")
## 311 complaint rows: 226069

Data Cleaning

Inspections

inspections <- raw_inspections |>
  mutate(
    inspection_date = as_date(inspection_date),
    score           = as.numeric(score),
    grade           = str_trim(grade),
    grade           = if_else(grade %in% c("A","B","C"), grade, NA_character_),
    is_critical     = str_detect(tolower(critical_flag), "critical") &
                      !str_detect(tolower(critical_flag), "not"),
    borough         = str_to_upper(str_trim(boro)),
    zipcode         = str_pad(str_extract(zipcode, "\\d{5}"), 5, "left", "0"),
    year            = year(inspection_date),
    month           = floor_date(inspection_date, "month"),
    camis           = as.character(camis)
  ) |>
  filter(
    !is.na(inspection_date),
    year >= 2022,
    borough %in% c("BRONX","BROOKLYN","MANHATTAN","QUEENS","STATEN ISLAND"),
    !is.na(zipcode),
    zipcode != "00000"
  ) |>
  select(
    camis, dba, borough, zipcode, cuisine_description,
    inspection_date, year, month, score, grade,
    violation_code, violation_description,
    critical_flag, is_critical, action, inspection_type
  )

# Quality check
cat("Clean inspection rows:", nrow(inspections), "\n")
## Clean inspection rows: 285460
cat("Date range:", as.character(min(inspections$inspection_date, na.rm=TRUE)),
    "to", as.character(max(inspections$inspection_date, na.rm=TRUE)), "\n")
## Date range: 2022-01-03 to 2026-04-29
cat("% critical violations:", percent(mean(inspections$is_critical, na.rm=TRUE)), "\n")
## % critical violations: 53%
cat("% grade A:", percent(mean(inspections$grade == "A", na.rm=TRUE)), "\n")
## % grade A: 75%

311 Complaints

complaints <- raw_311 |>
  mutate(
    date           = as_date(created_date),
    month          = floor_date(date, "month"),
    year           = year(date),
    borough        = str_to_upper(str_trim(borough)),
    zipcode        = str_pad(str_extract(incident_zip, "\\d{5}"), 5, "left", "0"),
    complaint_type = str_trim(complaint_type)
  ) |>
  filter(
    !is.na(date),
    year >= 2022,
    borough %in% c("BRONX","BROOKLYN","MANHATTAN","QUEENS","STATEN ISLAND"),
    !is.na(zipcode),
    zipcode != "00000"
  ) |>
  select(
    unique_key, complaint_type, descriptor,
    date, month, year, borough, zipcode
  )

cat("Clean complaint rows:", nrow(complaints), "\n")
## Clean complaint rows: 225190
complaints |> count(complaint_type, sort = TRUE) |> print()
## # A tibble: 4 × 2
##   complaint_type            n
##   <chr>                 <int>
## 1 Rodent               160737
## 2 Food Establishment    50604
## 3 Food Poisoning        12586
## 4 Unsanitary Condition   1263

Neighborhood-Level Aggregation

# Inspections aggregated per ZIP per month
insp_zip_month <- inspections |>
  group_by(zipcode, borough, month) |>
  summarise(
    n_inspections = n_distinct(paste(camis, inspection_date)),
    n_critical    = sum(is_critical, na.rm = TRUE),
    n_violations  = n(),
    pct_critical  = mean(is_critical, na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_score    = mean(score, na.rm = TRUE),
    pct_grade_a   = mean(grade == "A", na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

# Complaints aggregated per ZIP per month — wide format
comp_zip_month <- complaints |>
  count(zipcode, borough, month, complaint_type) |>
  pivot_wider(
    names_from   = complaint_type,
    values_from  = n,
    values_fill  = 0,
    names_prefix = "comp_"
  ) |>
  rename_with(~ str_replace_all(., " ", "_"), starts_with("comp_")) |>
  mutate(comp_total = rowSums(across(starts_with("comp_"))))

# Joined ZIP-month panel
zip_month <- insp_zip_month |>
  left_join(comp_zip_month, by = c("zipcode", "borough", "month")) |>
  mutate(across(starts_with("comp_"), ~ replace_na(.x, 0)))

cat("ZIP-month panel rows:", nrow(zip_month), "\n")
## ZIP-month panel rows: 8530

SQ 1 — Do Persistently High-Complaint Neighborhoods Have More Repeat Critical Violations?

Hypothesis: ZIP codes in the top quartile of sustained complaint volume will show disproportionately higher rates of repeat critical violations compared to low-complaint ZIPs.

# Identify ZIPs by average monthly complaint volume (sustained >= 6 months)
zip_complaint_profile <- comp_zip_month |>
  group_by(zipcode, borough) |>
  summarise(
    months_observed  = n(),
    avg_monthly_comp = mean(comp_total, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) |>
  filter(months_observed >= 6) |>
  mutate(
    comp_quartile  = ntile(avg_monthly_comp, 4),
    high_complaint = comp_quartile == 4
  )

# Repeat critical violations: same restaurant, same violation code, 2+ inspection dates
repeat_criticals <- inspections |>
  filter(is_critical, !is.na(violation_code)) |>
  group_by(camis, zipcode, violation_code) |>
  summarise(
    n_times_cited = n_distinct(inspection_date),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) |>
  filter(n_times_cited >= 2) |>
  group_by(zipcode) |>
  summarise(
    n_repeat_critical = n(),
    n_restaurants_with_repeat = n_distinct(camis),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

# Restaurants per ZIP for rate denominator
restaurants_per_zip <- inspections |>
  group_by(zipcode) |>
  summarise(n_restaurants = n_distinct(camis), .groups = "drop")

# Combine
sq1_data <- zip_complaint_profile |>
  left_join(repeat_criticals,    by = "zipcode") |>
  left_join(restaurants_per_zip, by = "zipcode") |>
  mutate(
    n_repeat_critical    = replace_na(n_repeat_critical, 0),
    repeat_critical_rate = n_repeat_critical / n_restaurants
  )

# Summary by quartile
sq1_summary <- sq1_data |>
  group_by(comp_quartile) |>
  summarise(
    n_zips                   = n(),
    avg_complaints_per_month = mean(avg_monthly_comp),
    avg_repeat_critical_rate = mean(repeat_critical_rate, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

sq1_summary |>
  kable(
    digits    = 2,
    caption   = "SQ1: Repeat Critical Violation Rate by Complaint Volume Quartile",
    col.names = c("Complaint Quartile", "ZIP Codes",
                  "Avg Monthly Complaints", "Avg Repeat Critical Rate")
  ) |>
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped","hover","condensed"))
SQ1: Repeat Critical Violation Rate by Complaint Volume Quartile
Complaint Quartile ZIP Codes Avg Monthly Complaints Avg Repeat Critical Rate
1 49 2.70 0.94
2 49 11.61 1.09
3 49 24.61 1.21
4 49 48.79 1.11 
sq1_summary |>
  mutate(quartile_label = case_when(
    comp_quartile == 1 ~ "Q1\n(Lowest)",
    comp_quartile == 2 ~ "Q2",
    comp_quartile == 3 ~ "Q3",
    comp_quartile == 4 ~ "Q4\n(Highest)"
  )) |>
  ggplot(aes(x = quartile_label,
             y = avg_repeat_critical_rate,
             fill = factor(comp_quartile))) +
  geom_col(width = 0.6, show.legend = FALSE) +
  geom_text(aes(label = round(avg_repeat_critical_rate, 2)),
            vjust = -0.5, fontface = "bold", size = 4.5) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("#A8DADC","#457B9D","#1D3557","#E63946")) +
  scale_y_continuous(expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.15))) +
  labs(
    title    = "Higher-Complaint Neighborhoods Have More Repeat Critical Violations",
    subtitle = "Avg repeat critical violations per restaurant, by complaint volume quartile (ZIP level)",
    x        = "Complaint Volume Quartile",
    y        = "Avg Repeat Critical Violations per Restaurant",
    caption  = "Sources: DOHMH Inspections, NYC 311 (2022–2026)"
  ) +
  theme_nyc()

# Welch t-test: Q1 vs Q4 repeat critical rates
sq1_test <- sq1_data |>
  filter(comp_quartile %in% c(1, 4), !is.na(repeat_critical_rate))

t_result <- t.test(
  repeat_critical_rate ~ comp_quartile,
  data        = sq1_test,
  alternative = "less"  # Q1 < Q4
)

tidy(t_result) |>
  kable(digits = 4,
        caption = "Welch t-test: Q1 vs Q4 repeat critical violation rates") |>
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped","condensed"))
Welch t-test: Q1 vs Q4 repeat critical violation rates
estimate estimate1 estimate2 statistic p.value parameter conf.low conf.high method alternative
-0.1758 0.9384 1.1142 -1.551 0.0634 53.5217 -Inf 0.0139 Welch Two Sample t-test less 
cat("\nInterpretation: Q4 neighborhoods have",
    ifelse(t_result$p.value < 0.05,
           "SIGNIFICANTLY more", "no significantly more"),
    "repeat critical violations than Q1 (p =",
    round(t_result$p.value, 4), ")\n")
## 
## Interpretation: Q4 neighborhoods have no significantly more repeat critical violations than Q1 (p = 0.0634 )

SQ 2 — Are Higher-Complaint Neighborhoods Associated with Worse Inspection Outcomes?

Hypothesis: At the ZIP level, higher food-related 311 complaint volume correlates with lower grade-A rates and higher (worse) mean inspection scores.

sq2_data <- zip_month |>
  group_by(zipcode, borough) |>
  summarise(
    total_complaints = sum(comp_total,    na.rm = TRUE),
    avg_score        = mean(mean_score,   na.rm = TRUE),
    avg_pct_critical = mean(pct_critical, na.rm = TRUE),
    avg_pct_grade_a  = mean(pct_grade_a,  na.rm = TRUE),
    n_months         = n(),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) |>
  filter(total_complaints > 0, !is.na(avg_score), n_months >= 6)

# Correlation matrix
sq2_cor_data <- sq2_data |>
  select(total_complaints, avg_score, avg_pct_critical, avg_pct_grade_a)

sq2_cors <- cor(sq2_cor_data, use = "pairwise.complete.obs")

ggcorrplot(
  sq2_cors,
  method   = "circle",
  type     = "lower",
  lab      = TRUE,
  lab_size = 4,
  colors   = c("#2A9D8F", "white", "#E76F51"),
  title    = "SQ2: Complaint Volume vs. Inspection Outcomes (ZIP level)"
) +
  theme_nyc()

ggplot(sq2_data,
       aes(x = total_complaints, y = avg_score, color = borough)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.65, size = 2.5) +
  geom_smooth(aes(group = 1), method = "lm", se = TRUE,
              color = "black", linewidth = 1, linetype = "dashed") +
  scale_x_log10(labels = comma) +
  scale_color_manual(values = borough_colors) +
  labs(
    title    = "More Complaints, Higher (Worse) Inspection Scores",
    subtitle = "Each point = one ZIP code. Higher score = worse outcome.",
    x        = "Total Food-Related 311 Complaints (log scale)",
    y        = "Average Inspection Score",
    color    = "Borough",
    caption  = "Sources: DOHMH Inspections, NYC 311 (2022–2026)"
  ) +
  theme_nyc()

sq2_model <- lm(avg_score ~ log1p(total_complaints) + borough,
                data = sq2_data)

tidy(sq2_model) |>
  kable(digits = 4,
        caption = "SQ2: OLS — Avg Inspection Score ~ log(Complaints) + Borough") |>
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped","condensed"))
SQ2: OLS — Avg Inspection Score ~ log(Complaints) + Borough
term estimate std.error statistic p.value
(Intercept) 13.6649 1.0298 13.2692 0.0000
log1p(total_complaints) 1.0751 0.1307 8.2252 0.0000
boroughBROOKLYN 1.7922 0.6806 2.6331 0.0092
boroughMANHATTAN 0.9752 0.6454 1.5110 0.1325
boroughQUEENS 1.9693 0.6351 3.1006 0.0022
boroughSTATEN ISLAND -0.3106 0.9253 -0.3357 0.7375 
glance(sq2_model) |>
  select(r.squared, adj.r.squared, p.value, nobs) |>
  kable(digits = 4, caption = "Model fit summary") |>
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped","condensed"))
Model fit summary
r.squared adj.r.squared p.value nobs
0.3436 0.3256 0 189

SQ 3 — Do Increases in Complaints Precede Worse Inspection Outcomes?

Hypothesis: A rise in food-related complaints in month T predicts worse inspection scores in months T+1, T+2, and T+3.

# Monthly complaint totals per ZIP
zip_comp_monthly <- comp_zip_month |>
  select(zipcode, month, comp_total)

# Monthly inspection outcomes per ZIP
zip_insp_monthly <- insp_zip_month |>
  select(zipcode, month, pct_critical, mean_score, pct_grade_a)

# Build lagged predictors: complaints in T predict outcomes in T+1, T+2, T+3
make_lag <- function(df, lag_months) {
  df |>
    mutate(month = month + months(lag_months)) |>
    rename_with(~ paste0(.x, "_lag", lag_months), comp_total)
}

sq3_data <- zip_insp_monthly |>
  left_join(make_lag(zip_comp_monthly, 1), by = c("zipcode","month")) |>
  left_join(make_lag(zip_comp_monthly, 2), by = c("zipcode","month")) |>
  left_join(make_lag(zip_comp_monthly, 3), by = c("zipcode","month")) |>
  filter(!is.na(pct_critical))

# Lagged correlations
lag_cors <- tibble(
  lag = c("Lag 1 month","Lag 2 months","Lag 3 months"),
  cor_pct_critical = c(
    cor(sq3_data$comp_total_lag1, sq3_data$pct_critical, use = "pairwise.complete.obs"),
    cor(sq3_data$comp_total_lag2, sq3_data$pct_critical, use = "pairwise.complete.obs"),
    cor(sq3_data$comp_total_lag3, sq3_data$pct_critical, use = "pairwise.complete.obs")
  ),
  cor_mean_score = c(
    cor(sq3_data$comp_total_lag1, sq3_data$mean_score, use = "pairwise.complete.obs"),
    cor(sq3_data$comp_total_lag2, sq3_data$mean_score, use = "pairwise.complete.obs"),
    cor(sq3_data$comp_total_lag3, sq3_data$mean_score, use = "pairwise.complete.obs")
  )
)

lag_cors |>
  kable(digits = 4,
        caption = "SQ3: Lagged Correlations — Complaints → Inspection Outcomes") |>
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped","condensed"))
SQ3: Lagged Correlations — Complaints → Inspection Outcomes
lag cor_pct_critical cor_mean_score
Lag 1 month 0.0287 0.0719
Lag 2 months 0.0306 0.0762
Lag 3 months 0.0278 0.0755 
lag_cors |>
  pivot_longer(starts_with("cor_"),
               names_to  = "outcome",
               values_to = "correlation") |>
  mutate(outcome = recode(outcome,
    "cor_pct_critical" = "% Critical Violations",
    "cor_mean_score"   = "Mean Inspection Score"
  )) |>
  ggplot(aes(x = lag, y = correlation, fill = outcome)) +
  geom_col(position = "dodge", width = 0.5) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linewidth = 0.8) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("#E76F51","#264653")) +
  labs(
    title    = "Do Complaint Spikes Predict Future Inspection Outcomes?",
    subtitle = "Correlation between complaints in month T and outcomes in T+N (ZIP level)",
    x        = "Complaint Lag Window",
    y        = "Pearson Correlation",
    fill     = "Outcome",
    caption  = "Sources: DOHMH Inspections, NYC 311 (2022–2026)"
  ) +
  theme_nyc()

# Panel regression controlling for ZIP fixed effects
sq3_model <- lm(
  pct_critical ~ comp_total_lag1 + comp_total_lag2 + comp_total_lag3 + factor(zipcode),
  data = sq3_data
)

# Show only the lag coefficients — suppress ZIP fixed effects
tidy(sq3_model) |>
  filter(str_detect(term, "comp_total_lag")) |>
  kable(digits = 5,
        caption = "SQ3: Panel regression (ZIP FE) — lagged complaints → % critical violations") |>
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped","condensed"))
SQ3: Panel regression (ZIP FE) — lagged complaints → % critical violations
term estimate std.error statistic p.value
comp_total_lag1 0.00010 0.00014 0.74656 0.45535
comp_total_lag2 0.00011 0.00015 0.70351 0.48176
comp_total_lag3 -0.00018 0.00014 -1.26497 0.20592

SQ 4 — Which Complaint Types Are Most Strongly Associated with Critical Violations?

Hypothesis: Rodent complaints will show the strongest positive correlation with critical violation rates, as rodent presence is a direct marker of sanitation failure.

# ZIP-level complaint counts by type
sq4_complaints <- complaints |>
  count(zipcode, complaint_type) |>
  pivot_wider(names_from  = complaint_type,
              values_from = n,
              values_fill = 0)

# ZIP-level inspection outcomes
sq4_inspections <- inspections |>
  group_by(zipcode) |>
  summarise(
    n_inspections = n_distinct(paste(camis, inspection_date)),
    pct_critical  = mean(is_critical, na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_score    = mean(score, na.rm = TRUE),
    pct_grade_a   = mean(grade == "A", na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) |>
  filter(n_inspections >= 10)

sq4_data <- sq4_inspections |>
  left_join(sq4_complaints, by = "zipcode")

# Complaint type columns present in data
complaint_cols <- intersect(
  names(sq4_data),
  unique(complaints$complaint_type)
)

# Correlation of each complaint type with outcomes
sq4_cors <- map_dfr(complaint_cols, function(ct) {
  vals <- sq4_data[[ct]]
  if (sum(!is.na(vals) & vals > 0) < 10) return(NULL)
  tibble(
    complaint_type   = ct,
    cor_pct_critical = cor(vals, sq4_data$pct_critical, use = "pairwise.complete.obs"),
    cor_mean_score   = cor(vals, sq4_data$mean_score,   use = "pairwise.complete.obs"),
    cor_pct_grade_a  = cor(vals, sq4_data$pct_grade_a,  use = "pairwise.complete.obs"),
    n_zips_with_data = sum(!is.na(vals) & vals > 0)
  )
}) |>
  filter(!is.na(cor_pct_critical)) |>
  arrange(desc(abs(cor_pct_critical)))

sq4_cors |>
  kable(digits = 4,
        caption   = "SQ4: Complaint Type Correlations with Inspection Outcomes",
        col.names = c("Complaint Type", "Cor w/ % Critical",
                      "Cor w/ Mean Score", "Cor w/ % Grade A", "ZIP Count")) |>
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped","hover","condensed"))
SQ4: Complaint Type Correlations with Inspection Outcomes
Complaint Type Cor w/ % Critical Cor w/ Mean Score Cor w/ % Grade A ZIP Count
Food Establishment 0.1572 0.1582 -0.1541 190
Rodent 0.1162 0.1334 -0.1826 183
Food Poisoning 0.1088 0.1289 -0.1075 188
Unsanitary Condition 0.0475 0.0976 -0.1770 146 
sq4_cors |>
  mutate(
    complaint_type = fct_reorder(complaint_type, cor_pct_critical),
    direction      = if_else(cor_pct_critical >= 0, "Positive", "Negative")
  ) |>
  ggplot(aes(x = cor_pct_critical, y = complaint_type, fill = direction)) +
  geom_col(show.legend = FALSE, width = 0.6) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linewidth = 0.9) +
  geom_text(aes(label = round(cor_pct_critical, 3),
                hjust = if_else(cor_pct_critical >= 0, -0.2, 1.2)),
            size = 4, fontface = "bold") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Positive" = "#E76F51", "Negative" = "#2A9D8F")) +
  scale_x_continuous(expand = expansion(mult = 0.2)) +
  labs(
    title    = "Which Complaint Types Best Predict Critical Violations?",
    subtitle = "Pearson correlation with % critical violations at ZIP level",
    x        = "Correlation with % Critical Violations",
    y        = NULL,
    caption  = "Sources: DOHMH Inspections, NYC 311 (2022–2026)"
  ) +
  theme_nyc()

SQ 5 — Do Complaints Lead to Inspections?

Hypothesis: Restaurants in ZIP codes that received a food-related 311 complaint are inspected sooner than the typical baseline inspection cycle.

# Baseline: typical gap between consecutive inspections (no complaint signal)
baseline_gaps <- inspections |>
  arrange(camis, inspection_date) |>
  group_by(camis) |>
  mutate(
    prev_inspection = lag(inspection_date),
    days_gap        = as.numeric(inspection_date - prev_inspection)
  ) |>
  filter(!is.na(days_gap), days_gap > 1, days_gap < 730) |>
  ungroup()

baseline_median <- median(baseline_gaps$days_gap, na.rm = TRUE)
baseline_mean   <- mean(baseline_gaps$days_gap,   na.rm = TRUE)

cat("Baseline median days between inspections:", round(baseline_median), "\n")
## Baseline median days between inspections: 315
cat("Baseline mean days between inspections:",   round(baseline_mean),   "\n")
## Baseline mean days between inspections: 302
sq5_matched <- readRDS("data/sq5_matched.rds")

post_complaint_median <- median(sq5_matched$days_to_inspection, na.rm = TRUE)

cat("Matched pairs:", nrow(sq5_matched), "\n")
## Matched pairs: 19871
cat("Post-complaint median days to inspection:", round(post_complaint_median), "\n")
## Post-complaint median days to inspection: 4
cat("Baseline median:", round(baseline_median), "\n")
## Baseline median: 315
cat("Days faster than baseline:",
    round(baseline_median - post_complaint_median), "\n")
## Days faster than baseline: 311
ggplot(sq5_matched, aes(x = days_to_inspection)) +
  geom_histogram(binwidth = 7, fill = "#264653",
                 color = "white", alpha = 0.85) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = post_complaint_median,
             color = "#E76F51", linewidth = 1.3, linetype = "dashed") +
  geom_vline(xintercept = baseline_median,
             color = "#2A9D8F", linewidth = 1.3, linetype = "dashed") +
  annotate("text",
           x = post_complaint_median + 4, y = Inf,
           vjust = 2, hjust = 0, color = "#E76F51", size = 3.5,
           label = glue("Post-complaint\nmedian: {round(post_complaint_median)} days")) +
  annotate("text",
           x = baseline_median + 4, y = Inf,
           vjust = 4.5, hjust = 0, color = "#2A9D8F", size = 3.5,
           label = glue("Baseline\nmedian: {round(baseline_median)} days")) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 180, 30)) +
  labs(
    title    = "Do Complaints Trigger Faster Inspections?",
    subtitle = "Days from 311 food complaint to next inspection in same ZIP (within 180 days)",
    x        = "Days from Complaint to Next Inspection",
    y        = "Number of Complaint-Inspection Pairs",
    caption  = "Sources: DOHMH Inspections, NYC 311 (2022–2026)"
  ) +
  theme_nyc()

sq5_matched |>
  group_by(complaint_type) |>
  summarise(
    n              = n(),
    median_days    = median(days_to_inspection),
    mean_days      = mean(days_to_inspection),
    pct_within_30d = mean(days_to_inspection <= 30),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) |>
  arrange(median_days) |>
  kable(
    digits    = 1,
    caption   = "SQ5: Time to Next Inspection by Complaint Type",
    col.names = c("Complaint Type","N Pairs","Median Days",
                  "Mean Days","% Inspected Within 30 Days")
  ) |>
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped","hover","condensed"))
SQ5: Time to Next Inspection by Complaint Type
Complaint Type N Pairs Median Days Mean Days % Inspected Within 30 Days
Food Poisoning 1152 3 6.8 1.0
Food Establishment 4461 4 7.1 1.0
Rodent 14144 5 8.9 0.9
Unsanitary Condition 114 5 7.0 1.0 
# Wilcoxon signed-rank test: is post-complaint inspection time < baseline?
wilcox_result <- wilcox.test(
  sq5_matched$days_to_inspection,
  mu          = baseline_median,
  alternative = "less"
)

cat("Wilcoxon test p-value:", round(wilcox_result$p.value, 5), "\n")
## Wilcoxon test p-value: 0
cat("Conclusion: post-complaint inspections ARE",
    ifelse(wilcox_result$p.value < 0.05, "", "NOT"),
    "significantly faster than baseline (p =",
    round(wilcox_result$p.value, 4), ")\n")
## Conclusion: post-complaint inspections ARE  significantly faster than baseline (p = 0 )

Overall Findings Summary

tibble(
  SQ       = paste("SQ", 1:5),
  Question = c(
    "Persistently high complaint volume → higher repeat critical violations?",
    "Higher complaint volume → worse inspection outcomes?",
    "Complaint increases precede worse outcomes?",
    "Which complaint types predict violations most?",
    "Complaints lead to faster inspections?"
  ),
  Method   = c(
    "Quartile grouping + Welch t-test",
    "OLS regression (score ~ log complaints + borough)",
    "Lagged correlation + panel regression (ZIP FE)",
    "Pearson correlation by complaint type",
    "Time-to-inspection vs baseline + Wilcoxon test"
  ),
Finding = c(
    "Directional trend: Q4 ZIP codes average 1.11 repeat critical violations per restaurant vs 0.94 in Q1, but difference is borderline (p = 0.063).",
    "Strong positive association: each unit increase in log(complaints) adds 1.08 points to inspection score (p < 0.0001, R2 = 0.34).",
    "Weak lagged signal: correlations positive but small (0.03-0.08) and panel regression coefficients non-significant. Complaints do not strongly predict future outcomes.",
    "Food Establishment complaints show strongest correlation with critical violations (r = 0.157), followed by Rodent (0.116), Food Poisoning (0.109), Unsanitary Condition (0.047).",
    "Strong confirmation: post-complaint median inspection is 4 days vs 315-day baseline. Wilcoxon p < 0.0001 — complaints dramatically accelerate inspections."
  )
) |>
  kable(caption = "Summary of All Specific Question Findings") |>
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped","hover")) |>
  column_spec(1, bold = TRUE, width = "4em") |>
  column_spec(4, italic = TRUE, color = "#E63946")
Summary of All Specific Question Findings
SQ Question Method Finding
SQ 1 Persistently high complaint volume → higher repeat critical violations? Quartile grouping + Welch t-test Directional trend: Q4 ZIP codes average 1.11 repeat critical violations per restaurant vs 0.94 in Q1, but difference is borderline (p = 0.063).
SQ 2 Higher complaint volume → worse inspection outcomes? OLS regression (score ~ log complaints + borough) Strong positive association: each unit increase in log(complaints) adds 1.08 points to inspection score (p < 0.0001, R2 = 0.34).
SQ 3 Complaint increases precede worse outcomes? Lagged correlation + panel regression (ZIP FE) Weak lagged signal: correlations positive but small (0.03-0.08) and panel regression coefficients non-significant. Complaints do not strongly predict future outcomes.
SQ 4 Which complaint types predict violations most? Pearson correlation by complaint type Food Establishment complaints show strongest correlation with critical violations (r = 0.157), followed by Rodent (0.116), Food Poisoning (0.109), Unsanitary Condition (0.047).
SQ 5 Complaints lead to faster inspections? Time-to-inspection vs baseline + Wilcoxon test Strong confirmation: post-complaint median inspection is 4 days vs 315-day baseline. Wilcoxon p < 0.0001 — complaints dramatically accelerate inspections.
